```
в”Ӯ  в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ    в”Ӯ                               в”Ӯ                           в”Ӯ
в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҙв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҳ

в•”в•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•җв•—

```

---

## Section Details

### Header (~4.5" tall)
| Element | Content |
|---|---|
| Title | "Correctness-Aware Caching for Agentic LLM Tool Calls" |
| Authors | Archit Kumar В· Maaz Hussain В· Divya Pothavajhyula В· Rohan Tolani |
| Affiliation | University of Michigan, CSE 585 |

---

### Left Column (~9" wide)

**Problem & Contribution (~8")**
- LLM agents call tools (price APIs, news feeds, etc.) sequentially to answer queries
- Caching reduces latency and API costs вҖ” but stale data corrupts multi-step reasoning
- A stale root-node result can route the agent down the wrong branch entirely
- Existing metrics (hit rate, staleness age) don't capture decision correctness
- We show: staleness impact depends on DAG position, and a workflow-aware TTL policy exploits this

**System Architecture (~6")**
Three-box diagram: `LangGraph Agent` вҶ’ `Cache Gateway (policy plug-in)` вҶ’ `API Simulator`  
Label the four APIs: `price`, `trend`, `weather`, `news_sentiment`

**Workflow 1: Investment Decision DAG (~7")**
```
       [get_price(ticker)]   вҶҗ 3 downstream deps вҶ’ tighter TTL
              вҶ“
    в”Ңв”Җв”Җв”Җ branch on price в”Җв”Җв”Җв”җ
    вҶ“                       вҶ“
[get_news_sentiment]    [get_trend]    вҶҗ 1 dep each вҶ’ relaxed TTL
    вҶ“                       вҶ“
 [SELL/HOLD]         [BUY/SELL/HOLD]
```

**Workflow 2: Portfolio Rebalancing DAG (~7")**
```
[get_price(AAPL)] в”Җв”ҖвҶ’ [compute_risk] в”Җв”Җв”җ
        в””в”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”ҖвҶ’ [compute_tax]  в”Җв”ҖвҶ’ [decide]  (AAPL: 3 deps)
[get_price(GOOG)] в”Җв”ҖвҶ’ [compute_risk] в”Җв”ҖвҶ’ [decide] (GOOG: 2 deps)
[get_price(NVDA)] в”Җв”ҖвҶ’ [compute_risk] в”Җв”ҖвҶ’ [decide] (NVDA: 2 deps)
```

---

### Center Column (~11" wide)

**Figure 1 вҖ” Hero: Mismatch Rate Comparison** (`hero_mismatch.png`, ~14")

*Callout above or below:* **"2.25Г— fewer errors (Investment) В· 2.0Г— fewer errors (Portfolio)"**

Explanation (2вҖ“3 sentences): Mismatch rate = cached-data decision вү  fresh-data decision. Both workflows use the same base TTLs вҖ” the only difference is whether the policy is blind to DAG structure or not. Workflow-aware cuts errors by ~2Г— in both despite fundamentally different topologies.

---

**Figure 2 вҖ” Latency-Correctness Tradeoff** (`latency_tradeoff.png`, ~9")

*Callout:* **"2.5Г— faster than no-cache, ~40% more latency than fixed TTL"**

Explanation (2 sentences): Workflow-aware sits in the sweet spot вҖ” far better correctness than fixed TTL at modest latency cost. It doesn't need to go all the way to no-cache to recover most of the accuracy.

---

**Figure 3 вҖ” Hit Rate вү  Correctness** (`hit_rate_disconnect.png`, ~8")

*Callout:* **"97% hit rate вҶ’ 6.5% decision errors (Portfolio fixed TTL)"**

Explanation (2 sentences): A high cache hit rate does not imply correct agent decisions in multi-step workflows. Optimizing for hit rate is the wrong objective вҖ” a cache can be nearly always hitting and still corrupt the majority of decisions where it matters.

---

**TTL Formula (~4")**

Styled formula box:
```
TTL = base_ttl / (dep_factor Г— pos_weight)

dep_factor = logвӮӮ(1 + downstream_deps)
pos_weight = 1.5 if workflow_step == 0, else 1.0
TTL floor  = 0.2 Г— base_ttl
```
One-line gloss: *Earlier nodes with more dependents get shorter TTLs вҖ” staleness there does the most damage.*

---

### Right Column (~9" wide)

**Figure 4 вҖ” Branch Breakdown** (`branch_breakdown.png`, ~9")

*Callout:* **"96% reduction on news_sentiment branch В· wrong-branch errors eliminated entirely"**

Explanation (2вҖ“3 sentences): Both branches route through the same stale root price node, but the news_sentiment branch has a tighter routing threshold вҖ” smaller price deviations cause wrong routing. Workflow-aware tightens the price TTL at the root, eliminating wrong-branch errors entirely. The trend branch sees a smaller gain (19%) because the threshold is looser.

---

**Results (~9")**

Investment Decision:
| Policy | Hit Rate | Mismatch Rate | Avg Latency |
|---|---|---|---|
| No Cache | 0% | 0.0% | 280ms |
| Fixed TTL | 79.9% | 2.7% | 79ms |
| Workflow-Aware | 49.6% | **1.2%** | 110ms |

Portfolio Rebalancing:
| Policy | Hit Rate | Mismatch Rate | Avg Latency |
|---|---|---|---|
| No Cache | 0% | 0.3% | 337ms |
| Fixed TTL | 97.1% | 6.5% | 89ms |
| Workflow-Aware | 91.6% | **3.2%** | 111ms |

---

**Conclusion (~4")**
- Staleness impact is non-uniform вҖ” DAG position matters more than change rate alone
- Workflow-aware TTL reduces decision errors by ~2Г— with only ~40% latency overhead vs. fixed TTL
- Hit rate is a poor proxy for correctness in agentic workflows

**Next Steps (~4")**
- Real LLM integration (currently uses deterministic decision rules)
- Adaptive TTL learning (currently hand-tuned per workflow)
- Multi-agent cache sharing analysis

---

- Optional: QR code linking to GitHub repo
